title: [Featurerequest] call Powershell and let response modify parameter
author: wolfgangp-tak
body_snippet:
Hello 

we are using a secure access tool to manage the access to RDP
- the tool creates a tunnel (opens a session on a dynamic localhost port)
- and also provides a password/username for every connection

It would be good to be able to execute a powershell script before each connections, which can modify the properties (username, password, protocol,.. more)

br
Wolfgang

iterations.description: Feature request body is empty with no implementation details, and the issue is stale with an outdated version label (1.77.4); cannot determine scope or approach without a concrete specification of what PowerShell integration should look like.
Wontfix: Pre-connect script hooks create security risks (arbitrary code execution) and error handling complexity

comments.author: Kvarkas
comments.snippet: This in my plans, should be implemented with connection profiles, then connection could be templated and then assign to a user with specific attributes or like in yours case fetch data from external tool and run connection (at this moment is refacturing a code what should not store connection data in mR app, what a prediction to implement profiles), but due amout of work, could expect in 1.77.4, also in 1.77.4 planing to add a can of worms - storage to place a scripts or scriptlets what could be...

comments.author: Kvarkas
comments.snippet: fyi: we have that chat space (Element) -> https://matrix.to/#/#mremoteng:matrix.org

notes: Feature: pre-connect PowerShell script to modify params. Requires scripting engine integration.
